nodes.label: Small-Server Optimizations
nodes.rationale: Caching, batched writes, connection pooling, a single worker, log rotation, and alert-log pruning keep the bot within a small-server resource budget.

nodes.label: Container Runtime Constraints
nodes.rationale: The bot is configured with automatic restart and a 512 MB memory limit so it can run reliably on a modest server.

nodes.label: Google OAuth Credential and Token Mounts
nodes.norm_label: google oauth credential and token mounts

nodes.label: Digest Notification Rationale
nodes.rationale: Operational events should be grouped into morning and afternoon digests to provide visibility while reducing alert fatigue; individual real-time alerts remain appropriate when a group first crosses the inactivity threshold.

nodes.label: Recommended Message Policy
nodes.rationale: Use real-time messages only for critical setup gaps, daily digests for routine monitoring, weekly summaries for broad review, and slash commands for on-demand checks.

nodes.label: Combined Message and Audit Inactivity Logic
nodes.norm_label: combined message and audit inactivity logic

nodes.label: Passive Audit Event Exclusion
nodes.rationale: Preview, open, download, copy, screenshot, and OCR events are excluded from the default reset list because passive consumption alone should not make a group active.

nodes.label: Weak Events Do Not Reset Inactivity
nodes.rationale: Passive navigation, consumption, screenshot, OCR, and similar weak audit events may appear in reports but should not reset inactivity by themselves.
nodes.norm_label: weak events do not reset inactivity
